$comment: Per-repo commit-gate rules for chonkers. Aligned with the PRD architecture: src/{persistence,schema,engine,ai,store,analytics,sim,audio,design} as pure-TS packages; app/{canvas,screens,components,input,hooks,boot,css} as the React layer. Tests co-located in __tests__/ within each package. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and docs/plans/*.prq.md for the full picture. Many rules below are PREVENTIVE — they fire only when a future commit introduces a violation. The fact that no current src/ files import 'react' or call Math.random() doesn't mean the rules are unused; it means the rules have been protecting the boundary from being crossed since they were authored.

coverage_rules.block_message: src/engine/ change without a co-located __tests__ update. The engine is the spine — every behavior change must land with the property test that proves it.
src/ai/ change without a co-located __tests__ update.
src/persistence/ change without a co-located __tests__ update.
src/schema/ change without a co-located __tests__ update. Migration changes must land with tests proving they apply correctly.
src/store/ change without a co-located __tests__ update.
src/analytics/ change without a co-located __tests__ update.
src/sim/ change without a co-located __tests__ update. The sim broker is the integration point — changes must land with the matching test.
app/ render/screen/component change requires browser-tier test pass evidence.

coverage_rules.or_explicit_skip: // rules-doc-only: <≥10 word reason explaining why no test follows>
coverage_rules.block_message: RULES.md is authoritative. A rule change must land with the engine test that proves it.
AI.md is authoritative. A contract change must land with the test that proves it.
Capacitor / Android / iOS change requires `pnpm cap:sync` evidence.

coverage_rules.or_explicit_skip: // new-migration: <≥10 word reason — only allowed for files matching ^[0-9]{3}-.*\.sql$ that did not previously exist>
coverage_rules.block_message: Schema migrations are forward-only. Existing .sql files MUST NOT be modified. Add a new NNN-name.sql file instead.

ban_patterns.message: chonkers AI is fully deterministic. The 'two identical games' fear is a flawed-algorithm symptom, not a determinism problem. No PRNG anywhere in game logic.
Engine + AI must be wall-clock independent. Animation timing belongs in app/ via R3F's useFrame.
src/ is pure TypeScript. React lives in app/.
src/ is pure TypeScript. ReactDOM lives in app/.
src/engine/ is a leaf package. It must not import from any other src/ package. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md module-boundary rules.
src/ai/ may only import from src/engine/ (one-way). See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md module-boundary rules.
src/persistence/ is generic transport. It must not import from any chonkers-specific package. Portability is the whole point.
Yuka's MathUtils / NavMesh / WanderBehavior / steering call Math.random(). Use only Yuka's deterministic primitives (Graph, Node, Edge, Vector3, BoundingSphere, BVH, Dijkstra, AStar).
src/ never imports from app/. The dependency arrow is one-way: app/ → src/.
